title: Recursive file system destruction via destructive command injection in agentic component
description: An agentic component embeds destructive file system commands — most commonly `rm -rf /` or `rm -rf ~` — within otherwise legitimate-looking setup or cleanup instructions. When an agent with file system or shell tool access follows these instructions, it permanently destroys files on the host. Variations target Windows (`del /s /q`, `rmdir /s /q`) and forks bombs (`:(){:|:&};:`). Losses are irrecoverable without backup.
behavioral_fingerprint: Component instructs the agent to execute recursive deletion commands on broad filesystem paths or system directories.
detection_methodology: 1. Static scan: search for rm -rf with root, home, or wildcard targets; Windows del/rmdir with /s flags; fork bomb patterns. 2. Semantic analysis: flag any 'cleanup' instruction that operates on paths outside a declared working directory. 3. Behavioral sandbox: monitor filesystem delete operations during agent execution.
indicators_of_compromise: Component contains rm -rf targeting /, ~, or * paths
Component presents destructive operation as a 'cleanup' or 'reset' step
Large-scale file deletion events observed in agent sandbox
Windows equivalent del /s /q or rmdir /s /q targeting drive roots
remediation: 1. Stop the agent immediately and disconnect from the host. 2. Restore from backup. 3. Audit all other components from the same source. 4. Review agent permission boundaries — agents should never have recursive delete access to system paths.
